title: React DevTools Integration
overview: Add react-devtools meta command with subcommands for React inspection. Lazy-downloaded hook injected on-demand via enable. No env var, no auto-inject.
scopeChallenge: next-browser implements this pattern. We adapt: lazy download instead of vendoring, CLI subcommands instead of programmatic API. No BROWSE_REACT_DEVTOOLS env var — purely on-demand.

failureModes.mitigation: Cache permanently. Error includes manual fallback: npm install -g react-devtools-core
Hook supports React 16+. Clear version error.
Tree/suspense work. Profiler needs profiling build. Document.
Inline React dev build in fixture HTML

tasks.title: Hook download + injection
tasks.description: Create src/react-devtools.ts with ensureHook(), injectHook(), removeHook(), isEnabled(). Add reactDevToolsEnabled flag to BrowserManager.
acceptanceCriteria: ensureHook downloads installHook.js from unpkg on first call
Second call uses cache (no network)
injectHook calls context.addInitScript with hook script
Download failure returns clear error with manual fallback

tasks.title: Query functions
tasks.description: Add query functions to src/react-devtools.ts: getTree, getProps, getSuspense, getErrors, getProfiler, getHydration, getRenders, getOwners, getContext. All via page.evaluate against __REACT_DEVTOOLS_GLOBAL_HOOK__. Reference: next-browser/src/tree.ts:69-175, suspense.ts:736-740, browser.ts:308-393.
acceptanceCriteria: getTree returns formatted component tree with indentation
getTree returns not enabled when hook missing
getTree returns No React detected on non-React pages
getProps returns props/state for a DOM element
getSuspense returns boundary status (resolved/pending)

tasks.title: react-devtools meta command
tasks.description: Add react-devtools to META_COMMANDS. Implement handler with subcommands: enable, disable, tree, props, suspense, errors, profiler, hydration, renders, owners, context. Add to SAFE_TO_RETRY.
acceptanceCriteria: react-devtools enable downloads hook, injects, reloads page
react-devtools disable clears flag
react-devtools tree returns component tree after enable
react-devtools props @e3 returns props/state
All subcommands error clearly when not enabled
Unknown subcommand returns usage

tasks.title: Integration tests
tasks.description: Create test/fixtures/react-app.html with inline React 18. Test enable/disable lifecycle, tree, props, non-React pages, not-enabled error.
acceptanceCriteria: react-devtools enable on React page succeeds
react-devtools tree returns component names
react-devtools tree without enable returns not enabled error
react-devtools tree on non-React page returns No React detected
react-devtools disable sets flag to false
All existing tests pass

tasks.title: Documentation
tasks.description: Update SKILL.md, README, CHANGELOG with react-devtools command and all 11 subcommands.
acceptanceCriteria: react-devtools documented with all subcommands
CHANGELOG has v1.1.0 entry
When to Use What table has React debugging entries
Quick reference shows enable → tree → props → disable flow
